perturbation.original_text: The Sales Particulars you will receive, and any subsequent commercial information relating to this business, are supplied on the strict understanding that it should be kept confidential and must not be copied or supplied to a third party without the consent of The Business Partnership (TBP).
perturbation.changed_text: The Sales Particulars you will receive, and any subsequent commercial information relating to this business, are supplied with the understanding that it may be shared with financial advisors and legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the sales particulars 'must not be copied or supplied to a third party without the consent of TBP'. The modified text allows sharing with financial advisors and legal counsel. This contradicts the earlier confidentiality requirement, as financial advisors and legal counsel are third parties. Now it is unclear if the recipient of the Sales Particulars can share without written consent or that financial advisors and legal counsel are an exception.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMS AND CONDITIONS The Sales Particulars you will receive, and any subsequent commercial information relating to this business, are supplied with the understanding that it may be shared with financial advisors and legal counsel. All recipients the Sales Particulars and any other details irrevocably agree:- 1. To treat all information contained herein, and as may subsequently be supplied, with complete confidentiality. 2. To make no approach directly to the Owner or the Business and or Property either personally, by telephone, or in writing without the consent of TBP. 3. To visit the Owner and/or the Business or Property only and strictly by prior appointment to be made and confirmed through the Office of TBP. 4. To make no approach to the Staff of the business or discuss the proposed sale with any staff member without the consent of the Owners. 5. To request and obtain any and all information required, including any financial information, solely through the Offices of TBP. 6. To submit any offer for the purchase of the Business and/or property directly through the Agents TBP and not directly to the Owner. Once TBPs NDA and Terms and Conditions have been signed, further negotiations are subject to you agreeing:- 1. That in the event of agreeing to purchase the Business and/or Property will pay to TBP a deposit as a sign of good faith amounting to 5% of the purchase price but subject to a minimum of £5,000. This deposit will be held by TBP in a Non-Interest Earning Client Account, and will be applied to the purchase price at the time of completion. This deposit will be paid "Subject to Contract" and will be refundable (subject to clauses 2 and 3 below). 2. In the event that you withdraw from the purchase without good reason and where such reason is not connected to the purchase of the business, then the deposit will be used to cover our Clients reasonable legal costs (up to a maximum of £2,500), such costs being subject to VAT, up to the point of withdrawal. 3. That if you withdraw for any reason once Heads of Terms or Memorandum of Sale has been prepared and issued you will be charged an Agents Fee of £695.00 plus VAT. The balance of the deposit shall be returned to you forthwith. Should the sale of the business be withdrawn by the Vendor for any reason, the deposit monies will be returned to you in full. 4. That upon an offer being accepted on a Leasehold proposition you will agree to supply to TBP the names and addresses of appropriate referees including Bankers to whom TBP can apply for a reference in order to seek the Landlords consent for an assignment of the current Lease or issue of a new Lease. Any information provided herein are for the general guidance of intended purchasers and tenants and do not form any part of an offer or contract. All descriptions and any other details are given without responsibility on the part of TBP or their vendor client and any intending purchasers should not rely on them as statements or representations of fact. All intending purchasers or tenants should satisfy themselves to the correctness of all any statements contained herein prior to making an offer by inspection or otherwise. Neither TBP nor their employees make or give any warranty whatsoever in relation to the business and or the property described herein. SIGNED………………………………………………. PRINT NAME……………………………………………….. POSITION………………………………………….. (Companies Only) DATE………………………………… Page 2 of 2
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits sharing sales particulars with third parties without consent. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing sharing with financial advisors and legal counsel. The contradicted text then states that all recipients of the Sales Particulars irrevocably agree to treat all information with complete confidentiality. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text permits sharing with specific third parties, while the contradicted text mandates complete confidentiality from all recipients, which include financial advisors and legal counsel. This meets the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' because it creates inconsistent obligations regarding confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: To treat all information contained herein, and as may subsequently be supplied, with complete confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: To treat most information contained herein, and as may subsequently be supplied, with reasonable confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates 'complete confidentiality.' The changed clause uses 'reasonable confidentiality'. This introduces a contradiction. 'Complete' implies an absolute duty, whereas 'reasonable' implies a duty subject to certain exceptions or limitations. Then if complete confidentiality is required, how does reasonable confidentiality impact the text?
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMS AND CONDITIONS The Sales Particulars you will receive, and any subsequent commercial information relating to this business, are supplied with the understanding that it may be shared with financial advisors and legal counsel. All recipients the Sales Particulars and any other details irrevocably agree:- 1. To treat most information contained herein, and as may subsequently be supplied, with reasonable confidentiality. 2. To make no approach directly to the Owner or the Business and or Property either personally, by telephone, or in writing without the consent of TBP. 3. To visit the Owner and/or the Business or Property only and strictly by prior appointment to be made and confirmed through the Office of TBP. 4. Once TBPs NDA and Terms and Conditions have been signed, further negotiations are subject to you agreeing:- 1. That in the event of agreeing to purchase the Business and/or Property will pay to TBP a deposit as a sign of good faith amounting to 5% of the purchase price but subject to a minimum of £5,000. The balance of the deposit shall be returned to you forthwith. Should the sale of the business be withdrawn by the Vendor for any reason, the deposit monies will be returned to you in full. 4. All intending purchasers or tenants should satisfy themselves to the correctness of all any statements contained herein prior to making an offer by inspection or otherwise. Neither TBP nor their employees make or give any warranty whatsoever in relation to the business and or the property described herein. SIGNED………………………………………………. PRINT NAME……………………………………………….. POSITION………………………………………….. (Companies Only) DATE………………………………… Page 2 of 2
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated 'complete confidentiality,' while the changed text mandates 'reasonable confidentiality.' This introduces a contradiction because 'complete' implies an absolute duty, whereas 'reasonable' implies a duty subject to certain exceptions or limitations. The contradicted text also requires 'reasonable confidentiality.' While seemingly aligned with the change, the original 'complete confidentiality' clause set a higher standard. The changed text weakens this standard, potentially conflicting with an understanding that some information should have been treated with complete confidentiality based on the original clause. Although the changed text is found in the contradicted section, the fact that the contract now lacks the original 'complete confidentiality' standard creates a contradiction. The explanation states, 'Then if complete confidentiality is required, how does reasonable confidentiality impact the text?' This explains why it is a valid contradiction, and 'YES' is assigned.

perturbation.original_text: That in the event of agreeing to purchase the Business and/or Property will pay to TBP a deposit as a sign of good faith amounting to 5% of the purchase price but subject to a minimum of £5,000. This deposit will be held by TBP in a Non-Interest Earning Client Account, and will be applied to the purchase price at the time of completion. This deposit will be paid "Subject to Contract" and will be refundable (subject to clauses 2 and 3 below).
perturbation.changed_text: That in the event of agreeing to purchase the Business and/or Property will pay to TBP a deposit as a sign of good faith amounting to 5% of the purchase price but subject to a minimum of £5,000. This deposit will be held by TBP in an Interest Earning Client Account, and all earned interest will be credited to TBP. This deposit will be paid "Subject to Contract" and will be refundable (subject to clauses 2 and 3 below).
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the deposit will be held in a 'Non-Interest Earning Client Account'. The modified clause states it will be held in an 'Interest Earning Client Account', and the interest will be credited to TBP. This creates a direct contradiction about whether the deposit earns interest and, if so, who benefits. In a legal dispute over who gets the interest, it is difficult to say which clause is right.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMS AND CONDITIONS The Sales Particulars you will receive, and any subsequent commercial information relating to this business, are supplied with the understanding that it may be shared with financial advisors and legal counsel. All recipients the Sales Particulars and any other details irrevocably agree:- 1. To treat most information contained herein, and as may subsequently be supplied, with reasonable confidentiality. 2. This deposit will be held by TBP in an Interest Earning Client Account, and all earned interest will be credited to TBP. This deposit will be paid "Subject to Contract" and will be refundable (subject to clauses 2 and 3 below). 2. In the event that you withdraw from the purchase without good reason and where such reason is not connected to the purchase of the business, then the deposit will be used to cover our Clients reasonable legal costs (up to a maximum of £2,500), such costs being subject to VAT, up to the point of withdrawal. 3. Any information provided herein are for the general guidance of intended purchasers and tenants and do not form any part of an offer or contract. All descriptions and any other details are given without responsibility on the part of TBP or their vendor client and any intending purchasers should not rely on them as statements or representations of fact. All intending purchasers or tenants should satisfy themselves to the correctness of all any statements contained herein prior to making an offer by inspection or otherwise. Neither TBP nor their employees make or give any warranty whatsoever in relation to the business and or the property described herein. SIGNED………………………………………………. PRINT NAME……………………………………………….. POSITION………………………………………….. (Companies Only) DATE………………………………… Page 2 of 2
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the deposit account from 'Non-Interest Earning' to 'Interest Earning' and stipulates that the interest will be credited to TBP. The contradicted text contains the changed text within it, and therefore has the same contradiction. It's a direct contradiction regarding whether the deposit earns interest and who benefits from it, creating a strong in-text contradiction, as the contract now contains conflicting information regarding the deposit's interest and ownership.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that you withdraw from the purchase without good reason and where such reason is not connected to the purchase of the business, then the deposit will be used to cover our Clients reasonable legal costs (up to a maximum of £2,500), such costs being subject to VAT, up to the point of withdrawal.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that you withdraw from the purchase without good reason and where such reason is not connected to the purchase of the business, then the deposit will be used to cover TBP's legal costs (up to a maximum of £2,500), such costs being subject to VAT, up to the point of withdrawal.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says the deposit will be used to cover 'our Client's reasonable legal costs' while the new text says it will be used to cover 'TBP's legal costs'. The term 'Client' is undefined, and now there is a contradiction on whose legal costs will be covered. It is not clear whether 'Client' refers to the vendor or to TBP themselves.

perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'our Client's reasonable legal costs' with 'TBP's legal costs.' The contradicted text also contains the changed text, which indicates that the deposit will be used to cover TBP's legal costs. The problem is that 'Client' is undefined, and now it is unclear whether 'Client' refers to the vendor or to TBP themselves. This creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction because it's not clear whose legal costs will be covered, leading to uncertainty and potentially unenforceable terms.
